comment_text.sn42.6:1.1: Pāvārika means “blanket-weaver”. Places were often named for their primary economic activity. Probably there was a community of blanket weavers and the park was owned by their leading merchant. See too note at MN 56:2.1.
comment_text.sn42.6:1.2: Asibandhaka’s son appears in each discourse up to SN 42.9. His father’s name “Swordstrap”, following on the previous discourses, suggests a military connection (cf. asibaddha, “sword-strapped”, Śāṅkhāyana Śrautasūtra 14.22.20); but the texts do not confirm this.
comment_text.sn42.6:2.1: The “western brahmins” (brāhmaṇā pacchā bhūmakā) hail from lands such as Kuru and Pañcālā or further west to Gandhārā. They are depicted as adherents of ritualism, in contrast with the brahmins of the lands of the Buddha, especially Kosala, who were more aligned with the contemplative and philosophical teachings of the Upaniṣads. This tension is felt in the Upaniṣads themselves, as Yājñavalkya  is accused of flouting the teachings of the Vedic scholars of Kuru and Pañcālā (Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 3.9.19). | A “pitcher” (kāmaṇḍalu) of water was carried at all times to ensure personal hygiene (Manu 4.36). | I have not been able to identify the practice of “garlanding with aquatic flowers” (sevālamālikā). The term appears a couple of times in the Harivaṁsa (śaivālamala), but of trees (55.53) or a disheveled woman (83.38), where it seems to mean “moss”. It seems unlikely that brahmins would smear moss on themselves. The commentary says, “Bright and early, having taken sevāla or lotus, etc. from the water, they make a decorative garland for the sake of identifying those who believe in purification by water.” The emphasis on collecting the flowers at dawn hints at a connection with the Sun, who rises from the ocean each day (cf. Sāhityakaumudī 10.258). The malicious yakkha of DN 23:23.13 intimates the presence of water with his garland of yellow lotus (kumudamāli); he appears on a trade journey, probably to the west. The plant was probably a kind of Vallisneria, which grows profusely underwater, sending its small white flowers to the surface.
comment_text.sn42.6:2.2: I take the particle nāma here as indicating wonder. | Resolve saññāpenti as saṁ yāpenti, “they send them well”.
comment_text.sn42.6:4.3: For “supplication” (āyācana) see too AN 5.43:3.1 and DN 13:16.2. | For anuparisakkeyya (“circumambulate”), see Sanskrit ṣvaṣk, “go, move”.
comment_text.sn42.6:4.6: The Buddha argues that personal action rather than religious ritual determines rebirth.
comment_text.sn42.6:7.1: At Chāndogya Upaniṣad 6.6.2–3, Uddālaka compares the ghee that arises as curd is churned to the mind.

root_text.sn42.6:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nāḷandāyaṁ viharati pāvārikambavane.
root_text.sn42.6:1.2: Atha kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.6:2.1: “brāhmaṇā, bhante, pacchā bhūmakā kāmaṇḍalukā sevālamālikā udakorohakā aggiparicārakā.
root_text.sn42.6:2.2: Te mataṁ kālaṅkataṁ uyyāpenti nāma saññāpenti nāma saggaṁ nāma okkāmenti.
root_text.sn42.6:2.3: Bhagavā pana, bhante, arahaṁ sammāsambuddho pahoti tathā kātuṁ yathā sabbo loko kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjeyyā”ti?
root_text.sn42.6:3.1: “Tena hi, gāmaṇi, taññevettha paṭipucchissāmi. Yathā te khameyya tathā naṁ byākareyyāsīti.
root_text.sn42.6:4.2: idhassa puriso pāṇātipātī adinnādāyī kāmesumicchācārī musāvādī pisuṇavāco pharusavāco samphappalāpī abhijjhālu byāpannacitto micchādiṭṭhiko.
root_text.sn42.6:4.3: Tamenaṁ mahājanakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:4.4: ‘ayaṁ puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjatū’ti.
root_text.sn42.6:4.6: api nu so puriso mahato janakāyassa āyācanahetu vā thomanahetu vā pañjalikā anuparisakkanahetu vā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjeyyā”ti?
root_text.sn42.6:5.1: “Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, puriso mahatiṁ puthusilaṁ gambhīre udakarahade pakkhipeyya.
root_text.sn42.6:5.2: Tamenaṁ mahā janakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:5.3: ‘ummujja, bho puthusile, uplava, bho puthusile, thalamuplava, bho puthusile’ti.
root_text.sn42.6:5.5: api nu sā puthusilā mahato janakāyassa āyācanahetu vā thomanahetu vā pañjalikā anuparisakkanahetu vā ummujjeyya vā uplaveyya vā thalaṁ vā uplaveyyā”ti?
root_text.sn42.6:5.7: “Evameva kho, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso pāṇātipātī adinnādāyī kāmesumicchācārī musāvādī pisuṇavāco pharusavāco samphappalāpī abhijjhālu byāpannacitto micchādiṭṭhiko.
root_text.sn42.6:5.8: Kiñcāpi taṁ mahā janakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:5.9: ‘ayaṁ puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjatū’ti, atha kho so puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya.
root_text.sn42.6:6.2: idhassa puriso pāṇātipātā paṭivirato adinnādānā paṭivirato kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato musāvādā paṭivirato pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato samphappalāpā paṭivirato anabhijjhālu abyāpannacitto sammādiṭṭhiko.
root_text.sn42.6:6.3: Tamenaṁ mahā janakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:6.4: ‘ayaṁ puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjatū’ti.
root_text.sn42.6:6.6: api nu so puriso mahato janakāyassa āyācanahetu vā thomanahetu vā pañjalikā anuparisakkanahetu vā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyyā”ti?
root_text.sn42.6:7.1: “Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, puriso sappikumbhaṁ vā telakumbhaṁ vā gambhīre udakarahade ogāhetvā bhindeyya. Tatra yāssa sakkharā vā kaṭhalā vā sā adhogāmī assa; yañca khvassa tatra sappi vā telaṁ vā taṁ uddhaṁ gāmi assa.
root_text.sn42.6:7.2: Tamenaṁ mahā janakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:7.3: ‘osīda, bho sappitela, saṁsīda, bho sappitela, adho gaccha, bho sappitelā’ti.
root_text.sn42.6:7.5: api nu taṁ sappitelaṁ mahato janakāyassa āyācanahetu vā thomanahetu vā pañjalikā anuparisakkanahetu vā osīdeyya vā saṁsīdeyya vā adho vā gaccheyyā”ti?
root_text.sn42.6:8.1: “Evameva kho, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso pāṇātipātā paṭivirato, adinnādānā paṭivirato, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato, musāvādā paṭivirato, pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato, pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato, samphappalāpā paṭivirato, anabhijjhālu, abyāpannacitto, sammādiṭṭhiko,
root_text.sn42.6:8.2: kiñcāpi taṁ mahā janakāyo saṅgamma samāgamma āyāceyya thomeyya pañjaliko anuparisakkeyya:
root_text.sn42.6:8.3: ‘ayaṁ puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjatū’ti, atha kho so puriso kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjeyyā”ti.
root_text.sn42.6:8.4: Evaṁ vutte, asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:

translation_text.sn42.6:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Nāḷandā in Pāvārika’s mango grove.
translation_text.sn42.6:1.2: Then Asibandhaka’s son the chief went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn42.6:2.1: “Sir, there are western brahmins garlanded with aquatic flowers who carry pitchers, immerse themselves in water, and serve the sacred flame.
translation_text.sn42.6:2.2: When someone has passed away, oh! they lift them up, send them well, and convey them to heaven.
translation_text.sn42.6:2.3: But what about the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha: is he able to ensure that the whole world will be reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm when their body breaks up, after death?”
translation_text.sn42.6:3.1: “Well then, chief, I’ll ask you about this in return, and you can answer as you like.
translation_text.sn42.6:4.2: Take an individual who kills living creatures, steals, and commits sexual misconduct. They use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re covetous, malicious, and have wrong view.
translation_text.sn42.6:4.3: A large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty, circumambulating them with joined palms and saying:
translation_text.sn42.6:4.4: ‘When this person’s body breaks up, after death, may they be reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm!’
translation_text.sn42.6:4.6: Would that person be reborn in heaven because of their supplications?”
translation_text.sn42.6:5.1: “Chief, suppose a person were to throw a broad rock into a deep lake.
translation_text.sn42.6:5.2: And a large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty, circumambulating it with joined palms, and saying:
translation_text.sn42.6:5.3: ‘Rise, good rock! Float, good rock! Float to shore, good rock!’
translation_text.sn42.6:5.5: Would that broad rock rise up or float because of their supplications?”
translation_text.sn42.6:5.7: “In the same way, take a person who kills living creatures, steals, and commits sexual misconduct. They use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re covetous, malicious, and have wrong view.
translation_text.sn42.6:5.8: Even though a large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty …
translation_text.sn42.6:5.9: when their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.sn42.6:6.2: Take an individual who doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, or commit sexual misconduct. They don’t use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re contented, kind-hearted, and have right view.
translation_text.sn42.6:6.3: And a large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty, circumambulating them with joined palms and saying:
translation_text.sn42.6:6.4: ‘When this person’s body breaks up, after death, may they be reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell!’
translation_text.sn42.6:6.6: Would that person be reborn in hell because of their supplications?”
translation_text.sn42.6:7.1: “Chief, suppose a person were to sink a pot of ghee or oil into a deep lake and break it open. Its shards and chips would sink down, while the ghee or oil in it would rise up.
translation_text.sn42.6:7.2: And a large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty, circumambulating it with joined palms and saying:
translation_text.sn42.6:7.3: ‘Sink, good ghee or oil! Descend, good ghee or oil! Go down, good ghee or oil!’
translation_text.sn42.6:7.5: Would that ghee or oil sink and descend because of their supplications?”
translation_text.sn42.6:8.1: “In the same way, take an individual who doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, or commit sexual misconduct. They don’t use speech that’s false, divisive, harsh, or nonsensical. And they’re contented, kind-hearted, and have right view.
translation_text.sn42.6:8.2: Even though a large crowd were to come together to offer up supplication and entreaty …
translation_text.sn42.6:8.3: when their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.”
translation_text.sn42.6:8.4: When he said this, Asibandhaka’s son the chief said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn42.6:8.6: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
